query: Show references to an unexplained respiratory illness in Wuhan across 2020 — before it was called COVID-19 — and how the coverage evolved through the WHO naming, the pandemic declaration, and the vaccine rollout.

payload.entity: References to the unexplained respiratory illness in Wuhan across 2020
payload.query: Show references to an unexplained respiratory illness in Wuhan across 2020 — before it was called COVID-19 — and how the coverage evolved through the WHO naming, the pandemic declaration, and the vaccine rollout.

representative_clip.title: Mh8UGRIOwLU.mkv
representative_clip.reason: Anchor and on-screen headline explicitly say 'WUHAN PNEUMONIA,' a pre-COVID-19 label tied to the unexplained outbreak.

scenes.title: Mh8UGRIOwLU.mkv
scenes.reason: Anchor and on-screen headline explicitly say 'WUHAN PNEUMONIA,' a pre-COVID-19 label tied to the unexplained outbreak.

scenes.title: ayUMPyMKcuI.mkv
scenes.reason: The introduction calls it a 'mysterious pneumonia outbreak' in Wuhan and visually pairs that with lung X-rays and protective gear before the COVID-19 name existed.

scenes.title: 9VQVHz8APLw.mkv
scenes.reason: The report links pneumonia symptoms to recent travel from Wuhan, showing the illness still framed as a location-based unexplained threat.

scenes.title: YhlEq5MqAkQ.mkv
scenes.reason: The segment uses a 'WUHAN VIRUS' graphic and hospital B-roll, reflecting early outbreak framing before official WHO naming.

representative_clip.title: GKymX9j_j9Q.mkv
representative_clip.reason: Tedros explicitly announces the official disease name 'COVID-19' and explains the spelling and rationale for the new terminology.

scenes.title: GKymX9j_j9Q.mkv
scenes.reason: Tedros explicitly announces the official disease name 'COVID-19' and explains the spelling and rationale for the new terminology.

representative_clip.title: O84hgvFk4HQ.mkv
representative_clip.reason: Tedros states on camera that COVID-19 can be characterized as a pandemic, making the narrative shift explicit.

scenes.title: O84hgvFk4HQ.mkv
scenes.reason: Tedros states on camera that COVID-19 can be characterized as a pandemic, making the narrative shift explicit.

scenes.title: 9mT0zFR9us8.webm
scenes.reason: This clip reinforces the declaration with case counts, spread, severity, and warnings about global inaction.

scenes.title: Unknown title
scenes.reason: The segment immediately pivots from the declaration to explaining what 'pandemic' means, showing the editorial escalation of the story.

representative_clip.title: p02fUBmOmlA.webm
representative_clip.reason: The UK rollout report shows Pfizer-BioNTech vaccinations beginning, marking the shift from emergency spread coverage to immunization.

scenes.title: p02fUBmOmlA.webm
scenes.reason: The UK rollout report shows Pfizer-BioNTech vaccinations beginning, marking the shift from emergency spread coverage to immunization.

scenes.title: WrmdYOuaY20.mkv
scenes.reason: The U.S. vaccine segment pairs first-dose expectations with factory vials and reminders that masks and distancing must continue.

payload.narrative_summary: The corpus shows a clear arc from January's 'mystery pneumonia' in Wuhan, to February's WHO naming, to March's pandemic framing, and finally December's vaccine rollout.

summary_bullets.headline: Wuhan mystery-illness framing
summary_bullets.text: January coverage is dominated by alarmed reports of a mysterious pneumonia in Wuhan, often explicitly using pre-COVID terminology like 'Wuhan pneumonia' or 'mysterious outbreak.'

summary_bullets.headline: Official naming standardizes the story
summary_bullets.text: February marks the vocabulary shift as WHO formally names the disease COVID-19, replacing looser Wuhan-centered descriptions with a global standard.

summary_bullets.headline: Outbreak becomes a pandemic story
summary_bullets.text: March coverage hardens into crisis reporting as Tedros says COVID-19 can be characterized as a pandemic, emphasizing spread, severity, and inaction.

summary_bullets.headline: Vaccines introduce cautious hope
summary_bullets.text: December coverage pivots to rollout logistics and public-health messaging, with early vaccinations framed as hopeful but not an immediate end to precautions.

estimated_value.calculation_basis: Proxy estimate based on 10 distinct broadcast clips in the retrieved corpus, valued at roughly $250 per distinct verified mention/scene for historical media-intelligence use.

inflection_points.label: Mystery illness emerges in Wuhan
inflection_points.why: Coverage frames the story as a mysterious or 'Wuhan pneumonia' outbreak, often before the disease had an official global name.

inflection_points.label: WHO names the disease COVID-19
inflection_points.why: The narrative shifts from geographic or symptom-based labels to a standardized WHO term intended to avoid stigma and unify reporting.

inflection_points.label: Pandemic declaration
inflection_points.why: Coverage moves from outbreak monitoring to full global-crisis framing once WHO says COVID-19 can be characterized as a pandemic.

inflection_points.label: Vaccine rollout begins
inflection_points.why: The story turns from spread and emergency response to first-dose logistics, prioritization, and cautious hope.
